iti48:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti48:2.1: “Monks, these two are doomed to deprivation, to hell, for not abandoning their conduct.
iti48:2.3:
One who, not living the celibate life, pretends to be one who lives the celibate life;
and one who groundlessly accuses one who lives the celibate life perfectly & purely of uncelibate behavior.
iti48:2.4: These are the two who are doomed to deprivation, to hell, for not abandoning their conduct.”
iti48:3.1: He goes to hell, the one who asserts what didn’t take place,
iti48:3.2:
as does the one who, having done, says,
‘I didn’t.’
iti48:3.4: after death, in the world beyond.
iti48:4.1: An ochre robe tied ’round their necks,
iti48:4.3: rearise, because of their evil acts,
iti48:5.1: Better to eat an iron ball
iti48:5.4: unrestrained, you should eat the alms of the country.
